text: "Just doing it" is the best teacher on figuring out what we don't know. I can say I absolutely love & adore your sharing on all the myriad of topics you share. Thank you for all the commitments you create. Cheers

author: @VeniceInventors
text:
Very well put. And no, the part about being honest with ourselves doesn't come out as arrogant, it's actually a rather gentle reminder that thinking about it isn't the same as doing something about it. 
Another aspect of creativity that would be worth covering is: Seeing things through, small step after small step, getting to the point where it's "done".  Except for the learning experience, the biggest waste of creativity is to start things and not finishing them. Which also robs the creator of that wonderful sense of accomplishment, not to mention the psychological weight of unfinished business which can prevent someone from moving on to the next project.

author: @koaasst
text: i wish kids today had that sense of wonder, but times have changed and the screen has taken over, the handheld device i mean

author: @ibme8359
text: I haven't watched Te-lie Vision such that I can't remember how many years it has been! Much of my time is spent gleaning ideas from you and others. Then I try to apply ideas I have learned. Seems like folks have forgotten how to apply themselves to any kind of creativity! They seem (to me) to be lost in idleness and conformity. I'm thankful for those such as you who know how to dig in (yes sometimes we need to adjust as we go along, but it's still a learning experience) and accomplish things! THANK YOU!!!

author: @TomFarrell-p9z
text: I would have the time, but I'm watching all these great videos!  🙂 I'll get back to doing soon; loving this new channel!  (Please consider adding the creation date of each, as Youtube is now showing the date it was moved here.)

replies.author: @traviswebster4622
replies.text: One of the biggest self-blocks I've noticed is the adding of unnecessary stipulations; we grow much more when we learn to declutter our lives and surroundings from all of the chatter and clamor. Simply doing is always best under any circumstances (especially when the doing is well thought out).
